message:
Those stiff little hairs on your dog's muzzle aren't just scruffy charm — they're one of the most amazing tools your pup owns. 🐾

They're called whiskers (vibrissae, if you want to sound fancy), and they're nothing like regular fur. Each one is rooted deep in the skin and wired to as many as 200 nerve endings — about ten times what an ordinary hair has. As your dog moves, those whiskers read the faintest puffs of air bouncing off walls and objects, building a kind of 3D map of the space around them. It's how they judge whether a gap is wide enough to squeeze through, and how they navigate a dark room without bumping into a thing.

So here's the important part: please never trim your dog's whiskers. They're not decoration — they're a sensory GPS your dog relies on every single day. 💛

Take a peek at your dog right now — how impressive are those whiskers? Tell me below! 🐶
